headline: Static audit surfaces 2 high-severity reproducibility risks (no pinned dependencies; checkpoints omit GradScaler + RNG state) plus numerous architectural and evaluation-protocol mismatches between the from-scratch 124M reproduction and the baseline table — but the Validator timed out, so no claim was runtime-confirmed.
executive_summary:
**Verdict: INCONCLUSIVE.** The Validator timed out (0 results, 0 seconds of runtime), so none of nanoGPT's four headline numbers — val loss 1.4697 on Shakespeare-char (claim_metrics_003), 1.88 on CPU (claim_metrics_004), ~2.85 on OpenWebText (claim_metrics_001), and the 4-entry GPT-2 baseline table — has been reproduced end-to-end in this audit. The static analysis is however strong and self-consistent, and every finding below traces to a quoted file:line in the repo.

The most consequential static findings: (1) No pinned dependencies anywhere (README.md:22 bare pip install). (2) Checkpoints omit GradScaler and all RNG state (train.py:277-284). (3) From-scratch 124M uses bias=False while baseline-table GPT-2 forces bias=True. (4) Baseline table is a noisy Monte-Carlo estimate over ~4.1M sampled tokens. (5) Only torch is seeded (1337); numpy/python/cudnn untouched and TF32 enabled.

**What to do first**: pin an environment snapshot, re-run Shakespeare-char twice to measure variance, and increase eval_iters by 10× for the OWT baseline table.

findings.title: No dependency pin file; README installs libraries with no version constraints
findings.description: No requirements.txt / pyproject.toml / setup.py / environment.yml exists in the repo. README.md:22 is the only install instruction and pins nothing. Code relies on PyTorch ≥ 2.0-only APIs (SDPA at model.py:45; torch.compile at train.py:208), fused AdamW (model.py:281), and the HuggingFace datasets legacy OpenWebText loader.

findings.title: Checkpoint omits GradScaler state and all RNG state; resume is not bitwise deterministic
findings.description: train.py:277-284 saves only {model, optimizer, model_args, iter_num, best_val_loss, config}. Missing: scaler.state_dict() (train.py:196), torch.get_rng_state()/torch.cuda.get_rng_state_all(), and numpy/random state. On resume, train.py:106 re-seeds torch to 1337+seed_offset regardless of iter_num, so post-resume data sequence does NOT match an uninterrupted run.

findings.title: From-scratch 124M reproduction uses bias=False while OpenAI GPT-2 has bias=True throughout
findings.description: The headline reproduction command inherits train.py:56 default `bias = False`; config/train_gpt2.py does not override. GPT.from_pretrained (model.py:222-225) forces bias=True for every OpenAI checkpoint used in the baseline table.

findings.title: Baseline-table losses are Monte-Carlo estimates over random windows, not full-split sweeps
findings.description: estimate_loss (train.py:215-228) averages loss.item() over eval_iters (=500) random batches. For the OWT val split (~4.4M tokens), 500×8×1024 ≈ 4.1M tokens sampled ≈ full-coverage-with-replacement — noisy, not deterministic. The 2-decimal-digit entries in the baseline table are not statistically resolved to their stated precision.

findings.title: Only torch RNG seeded; numpy/python/cudnn/CUBLAS untouched; TF32 enabled
findings.description: train.py:106 calls torch.manual_seed(1337+seed_offset). numpy.random.seed/random.seed never called. cudnn.deterministic never set; TF32 explicitly enabled at train.py:107-108. CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG not exported. 4-decimal-digit claims (1.4697, 1.88) are unreachable to that precision across machines.

findings.title: best_val_loss is overwritten unconditionally when always_save_checkpoint=True
findings.description: train.py:274-276 gates save with `if losses['val'] < best_val_loss or always_save_checkpoint:` and then unconditionally does `best_val_loss = losses['val']`. With always_save_checkpoint=True (default), the saved 'best_val_loss' is the most-recent val loss, not the minimum.

findings.title: val split is both the model-selection signal and the reported metric; no held-out test split exists
findings.description: All three prepare.py scripts produce only train.bin+val.bin. train.py:219 and train.py:274 both consume 'val'. The checkpoint is selected on val loss and the reported headline number is read off val — classic multi-testing issue.
